title: Add all possible container status states to the podman-ps manual page.
body:
Also remove the invalid 'stopped' state that was listed.

I found the full list of states from docker [here](https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/b59ee9486fad5fa19f3d0af0eb6c5ce100eae0fc/container/state.go#L150). I could not find if they were all also defined in libpod somewhere and assumed they were since libpod aims to mirror docker in functionality.

This PR (1) documents all of the available status codes, which I found hard to find anywhere besides the source code, and (2) the originally suggested `stopped` state is not a real state that can be queried.

```
$ podman ps -f name=rhel7 -f "status=stopped"
invalid filter: stopped is not a valid status
```

comments.body:
Hi @ekultails. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`
I don't know if we support `removing` and `restarting` - we don't have explicit internal states for these.

bot, add author to whitelist
/ok-to-test
If we can get confirmation of that, I can update the patch and remove those two states from this patch.
@ekultails I believe this is the complete list of output statuses we support: https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/993b3a17c58826365136f49d7760f8ac88e320b2/pkg/adapter/pods_remote.go#L246-L257

@mheon Thank you so much for helping dig that up. I also did some manually testing and can confirm that `dead` and `removing` are invalid.

Although not listed, in libpod, querying for the `restart` state does *seem* to work (behind the scenes I'm not sure if it really does).

```
$ podman ps -f name=rhel8 -f "status=restarting"
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE  COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS  NAMES
```

Based on your feedback, I will also have to note the `configured` state.

As for the CI/gating tests, it seems to be failing here and I'm unsure what the message `does not have a valid DCO` means. Is there something else I should be changing with my patch?

```
 * 5ffcfb15 "Add all possible container status states to the podman-ps manual page." ... FAIL
  - PASS - commit does not have any whitespace errors
  - FAIL - does not have a valid DCO
  - PASS - commit subject is 72 characters or less! *yay*
```

Hmm, it is also odd that `stopped` is listed but does not seem to work. Maybe I should open up an actual bug report for that.

```
$ rpm -qa | grep podman
podman-1.0.0-1.git82e8011.fc29.x86_64
$ podman ps -f name=rhel7 -f "status=stopped"
invalid filter: stopped is not a valid status
```
@ekultails Ah, you're missing a signoff - `git commit -s --amend` to add one, then re-push
Stopped not being recognized sounds like it could be a bug.

Thank you again for all of your help!

I have opened up a bug report [here](https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/2526) and will revisit this PR once more information is found about what should and should not work in regards to using `podman ps --format status=`.
Nice change, would be nice to have a test for each of these states, I'm not sure that we do.  But not required for this PR.  A bunch of test unhappiness.

I've located where the states are defined. I believe we intended to support the states originally used by Docker, but as I've noted some of these (restarting, etc) don't map to anything inside `libpod`.

As such, I'm going to alter the state list to the following:
- `created` (will include ContainerStateConfigured - they're so similar that it doesn't make sense to distinguish)
- `running`
- `paused`
- `exited` (will include ContainerStateStopped - again, so similar that it doesn't make sense to distinguish)
- `unknown` (Containers should never get into this state, but I'll include it for debug purposes)
#2586 to fix state filtering

@ekultails are you still working on this one?
@mheon @edsantiago @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL
Once #2732 merges this should be good to proceed.

Thank you for doing this: I'm a big fan of man pages enumerating lists of options. LGTM, and my inclination is to omit `stopped` even after #2732 merges, because the status shown by ps itself is `Exited`.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2697) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

I have rebased my patch the other day but a CI failure occurred with [rootless_testing](https://github.com/containers/libpod/runs/84561558). I don't believe these are related to my patch, rather, something else that was part of the rebase.
That one should be fixed upstream as of this morning - if it's still failing another rebase will pick up the fix
/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *ekultails*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@ekultails Can you rebase to pick up CI fixes? We should be able to merge once that's done
@mheon Thanks for the clarification. I rebased my patch this morning. Let me know if you need anything else from my end!

LGTM on my end.
@TomSweeneyRedHat @baude @rhatdan PTAL
